reasoning: The FRED trend and seasonal analysis provided a combined statistical estimate of P(increase) = 0.68 for the near-term date, pointing to mean reversion after the yield fell to 3.76% in anticipation of the 98% expected 25 bps rate cut on October 29. I used this 0.68 estimate as my primary probability anchor for the first resolution date (2025-11-02), as the market may experience a "buy the rumor, sell the fact" bounce or react to any hawkish cues in the Fed's forward guidance. For the subsequent resolution dates (2025-11-25 and 2026-01-24), it becomes less clear if the Fed will pause rate cuts or continue easing into early 2026. Therefore, I adjusted the probabilities downward closer to 0.5 (0.61 and 0.54, respectively) to reflect increasing uncertainty over longer forecast horizons as instructed.
